fields.Text:
Known Hodgkin lymphoma with thoracic and paraaortic adenopathy and splenic involvement. Lugano stage?

{{c1::Stage III (2)}}
fields.Extra: Q1. Correct; difficulty: hard. The imaging findings above are consistent with a large number of abnormal paraaortic lymph nodes (yellow arrows) as well as lower mediastinal nodes (green arrows).

fields.Text: In Lugano lymphoma staging, paraaortic nodal disease with splenic involvement is classified as {{c1::stage III (2)}}.
fields.Extra: Q1. Correct; difficulty: hard. The imaging findings above are consistent with a large number of abnormal paraaortic lymph nodes (yellow arrows) as well as lower mediastinal nodes (green arrows).

fields.Text:
6-week-old infant with prenatal posterior fossa abnormality. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Mega cisterna magna}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q2. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Mega cisterna magna shows normal cerebellar vermis, normal fourth ventricle, and enlarged posterior fossa fluid cavity without hydrocephalus. The cerebellar falx remains normal and there is physiological communication with subarachnoid space

fields.Text: Mega cisterna magna shows an enlarged posterior fossa CSF space with a {{c1::normal vermis and fourth ventricle and no hydrocephalus}}.
fields.Extra: Q2. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Mega cisterna magna shows normal cerebellar vermis, normal fourth ventricle, and enlarged posterior fossa fluid cavity without hydrocephalus. The cerebellar falx remains normal and there is physiological communication with subarachnoid space

fields.Text:
What prior breast procedure produces this expected mammographic appearance?

{{c1::Reduction mammoplasty}}
fields.Extra: Q3. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Reduction mammoplasty creates characteristic inferior "sweeping" architectural distortion from tissue removal and rearrangement that should remain stable - progressive changes warrant investigation for malignancy.

fields.Text: The expected post-surgical mammographic pattern of inferior sweeping architectural distortion is associated with {{c1::reduction mammoplasty}}.
fields.Extra: Q3. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Reduction mammoplasty creates characteristic inferior "sweeping" architectural distortion from tissue removal and rearrangement that should remain stable - progressive changes warrant investigation for malignancy.

fields.Text: Protection of the rights and welfare of human research subjects is the principal role of an {{c1::Institutional Review Board (IRB)}}.
fields.Extra: Q4. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: An Institutional Review Board serves an important role in the protection of the rights and welfare of human research subjects.

fields.Text:
Obtunded adult with medial temporal and insular abnormalities on MRI. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::HSV encephalitis}}

1/3
fields.Extra: Q5. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Herpes simplex (HSV) encephalitis is the most common cause of fatal sporadic fulminant necrotizing viral encephalitis and has characteristic imaging findings, including bilateral asymmetrical involvement of the limbic system, medial temporal lobes, insular cortices, and inferolateral frontal lobes. The basal ganglia are typically spared.

fields.Text: Adult sporadic necrotizing encephalitis involving the medial temporal lobes and insula, usually sparing the basal ganglia, is classically caused by {{c1::HSV-1}}.
fields.Extra: Q5. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Herpes simplex (HSV) encephalitis is the most common cause of fatal sporadic fulminant necrotizing viral encephalitis and has characteristic imaging findings, including bilateral asymmetrical involvement of the limbic system, medial temporal lobes, insular cortices, and inferolateral frontal lobes. The basal ganglia are typically spared.

fields.Text: In an adult with sporadic necrotizing temporal lobe encephalitis, the typical causative herpesvirus is {{c1::HSV-1}}, not HSV-2.
fields.Extra: Q5. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Herpes simplex (HSV) encephalitis is the most common cause of fatal sporadic fulminant necrotizing viral encephalitis and has characteristic imaging findings, including bilateral asymmetrical involvement of the limbic system, medial temporal lobes, insular cortices, and inferolateral frontal lobes. The basal ganglia are typically spared.

fields.Text:
Previously treated nasopharyngeal carcinoma with new cranial nerve palsies and skull-base disease. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Recurrent nasopharyngeal carcinoma encasing the right ICA}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q6. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: HMPAO imaging identifies baseline cerebral perfusion patterns and vascular territories at risk before tumor resection, allowing surgeons to modify their approach to preserve critical circulation. This method helps prevent inadvertent compromise of already hypoperfused brain regions during vessel manipulation or temporary occlusion.

fields.Text: When a skull-base tumor encases the ICA, a balloon occlusion test with Tc-99m HMPAO perfusion imaging assesses whether {{c1::collateral cerebral perfusion is sufficient to sacrifice the ICA during resection}}.
fields.Extra: Q6. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: HMPAO imaging identifies baseline cerebral perfusion patterns and vascular territories at risk before tumor resection, allowing surgeons to modify their approach to preserve critical circulation. This method helps prevent inadvertent compromise of already hypoperfused brain regions during vessel manipulation or temporary occlusion.

fields.Text: A recurrent skull-base nasopharyngeal carcinoma encasing the ICA is evaluated with balloon occlusion and HMPAO perfusion imaging to determine {{c1::whether the involved ICA can be sacrificed for resection}}.
fields.Extra: Q6. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: HMPAO imaging identifies baseline cerebral perfusion patterns and vascular territories at risk before tumor resection, allowing surgeons to modify their approach to preserve critical circulation. This method helps prevent inadvertent compromise of already hypoperfused brain regions during vessel manipulation or temporary occlusion.

fields.Text:
Asymptomatic patient with pubic symphyseal sclerosis and irregularity. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Osteitis pubis}}
fields.Extra: Q7. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Osteitis pubis shows irregular, sclerotic symphyseal margins with possible subchondral cysts on radiographs, representing noninfectious inflammation of the pubic symphysis. In asymptomatic patients, these degenerative-appearing changes are often incidental findings that don't require treatment, distinguishing it from symptomatic athletic pubalgia or infectious osteomyelitis which shows more extensive bone marrow edema and soft-tissue involvement.

fields.Text: Incidental sclerosis and irregularity of the pubic symphysis are characteristic of {{c1::osteitis pubis}}.
fields.Extra: Q7. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Osteitis pubis shows irregular, sclerotic symphyseal margins with possible subchondral cysts on radiographs, representing noninfectious inflammation of the pubic symphysis. In asymptomatic patients, these degenerative-appearing changes are often incidental findings that don't require treatment, distinguishing it from symptomatic athletic pubalgia or infectious osteomyelitis which shows more extensive bone marrow edema and soft-tissue involvement.

fields.Text:
What named distal radius fracture is shown?

{{c1::Type II Smith fracture (reverse Barton fracture)}}
fields.Extra: Q8. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Distal radius fracture with volar angulation and intra-articular extension is a reverse Barton (or type II Smith) fracture.

fields.Text: An intra-articular distal radius fracture with volar angulation is a {{c1::type II Smith fracture (reverse Barton fracture)}}.
fields.Extra: Q8. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Distal radius fracture with volar angulation and intra-articular extension is a reverse Barton (or type II Smith) fracture.

fields.Text: A distal radius fracture with intra-articular extension and volar angulation is a {{c1::type II Smith fracture (reverse Barton fracture)}}, rather than a dorsal Barton fracture.
fields.Extra: Q8. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Distal radius fracture with volar angulation and intra-articular extension is a reverse Barton (or type II Smith) fracture.

fields.Text: Because CT dose changes strongly with tube voltage, decreasing kVp from 140 to 100 can reduce dose by {{c1::more than half}}.
fields.Extra: Q9. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: On CT scanning, dose is directly proportional to mAs and kVp and inversely related to pitch. Decreasing dose has obvious advantages regarding patient radiation exposure; however, image noise must also be considered for each clinical indication to determine if the exam parameters will allow for diagnostic images to be obtained using the low-dose protocol.

fields.Text:
Voiding radionuclide cystogram in an infant with recurrent UTIs. What grade of reflux is shown?

{{c1::RNC grade I}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q10. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Radionuclide cystography offers highly sensitive vesicoureteral reflux detection with significantly lower radiation dose than contrast cystography. This makes it ideal for serial follow-up in pediatric patients to assess spontaneous resolution or to monitor treatment response.

fields.Text: On radionuclide cystography, reflux activity limited to the ureter is {{c1::RNC grade I}}.
fields.Extra: Q10. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Radionuclide cystography offers highly sensitive vesicoureteral reflux detection with significantly lower radiation dose than contrast cystography. This makes it ideal for serial follow-up in pediatric patients to assess spontaneous resolution or to monitor treatment response.

fields.Text: In the radionuclide reflux classification, tracer confined to the ureter is {{c1::grade I}}, while extension into the collecting system is grade II.
fields.Extra: Q10. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Radionuclide cystography offers highly sensitive vesicoureteral reflux detection with significantly lower radiation dose than contrast cystography. This makes it ideal for serial follow-up in pediatric patients to assess spontaneous resolution or to monitor treatment response.

fields.Text: An elective ACR premedication regimen for prior iodinated contrast reaction is prednisone 50 mg PO at {{c1::13, 7, and 1 hours}} before contrast, with diphenhydramine 50 mg 1 hour before.
fields.Extra: Q11. Incorrect; difficulty: easy. The ACR Manual on Contrast Media recommends prednisone PO 50mg 13 hours, 7 hours, and 1 hour prior to exam as well as Benadryl 50mg 1 hour before exam in cases of elective contrast-enhanced exams.

fields.Text: The elective oral prednisone premedication schedule for prior iodinated contrast reaction is {{c1::50 mg at 13, 7, and 1 hours before the exam}}.
fields.Extra: Q11. Incorrect; difficulty: easy. The ACR Manual on Contrast Media recommends prednisone PO 50mg 13 hours, 7 hours, and 1 hour prior to exam as well as Benadryl 50mg 1 hour before exam in cases of elective contrast-enhanced exams.

fields.Text: An incomplete or open ring of enhancement is relatively specific for {{c1::demyelination}}.
fields.Extra: Q12. Correct; difficulty: easy. The open ring sign is a relatively specific sign for demyelination, most commonly multiple sclerosis (MS).

fields.Text:
What is the most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Medullary nephrocalcinosis}}
fields.Extra: Q13. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Medullary nephrocalcinosis appears as high-attenuation calcifications within the renal pyramids on noncontract CT, representing the most common form of nephrocalcinosis and typically involving the renal papillae.

fields.Text: Calcifications centered in the renal pyramids are characteristic of {{c1::medullary nephrocalcinosis}}.
fields.Extra: Q13. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Medullary nephrocalcinosis appears as high-attenuation calcifications within the renal pyramids on noncontract CT, representing the most common form of nephrocalcinosis and typically involving the renal papillae.

fields.Text:
57-year-old nonsmoking woman with chronic dyspnea and expiratory HRCT abnormalities. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::DIPNECH}}

1/3
fields.Extra: Q14. Correct; difficulty: hard. Diffuse idiopathic neuroendocrine cell hyperplasia (DIPNECH) is defined by proliferation of neuroendocrine cells within the lung parenchyma.

fields.Text: Mosaic attenuation from air trapping with multiple pulmonary nodules in a nonsmoking woman with chronic dyspnea suggests {{c1::DIPNECH}}.
fields.Extra: Q14. Correct; difficulty: hard. Diffuse idiopathic neuroendocrine cell hyperplasia (DIPNECH) is defined by proliferation of neuroendocrine cells within the lung parenchyma.

fields.Text:
Delayed sestamibi planar image with persistent focal uptake. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Parathyroid adenoma}}
fields.Extra: Q15. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Sestamibi uptake occurs in both thyroid and parathyroid tissues. Abnormal parathyroid tissue retains tracer longer than normal thyroid on these examinations. Early images show both structures. Delayed images demonstrate differential washout with persistent focal uptake in adenomas while normal thyroid clears. This retention pattern enables adenoma localization.

fields.Text: Persistent focal uptake on delayed sestamibi imaging after thyroid washout localizes a {{c1::parathyroid adenoma}}.
fields.Extra: Q15. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Sestamibi uptake occurs in both thyroid and parathyroid tissues. Abnormal parathyroid tissue retains tracer longer than normal thyroid on these examinations. Early images show both structures. Delayed images demonstrate differential washout with persistent focal uptake in adenomas while normal thyroid clears. This retention pattern enables adenoma localization.

fields.Text:
What vascular compression syndrome is shown?

{{c1::Median arcuate ligament syndrome}}
fields.Extra: Q16. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: DSA demonstrates characteristic "hooked" appearance of the celiac artery origin on lateral views due to compression by the median arcuate ligament, often with post-stenotic dilation and prominent pancreaticoduodenal collateral circulation from the superior mesenteric artery.

fields.Text: A hooked celiac origin from extrinsic compression by the median arcuate ligament indicates {{c1::median arcuate ligament syndrome}}.
fields.Extra: Q16. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: DSA demonstrates characteristic "hooked" appearance of the celiac artery origin on lateral views due to compression by the median arcuate ligament, often with post-stenotic dilation and prominent pancreaticoduodenal collateral circulation from the superior mesenteric artery.

fields.Text: A transported Tl-201 package with an external wipe test below {{c1::20,000 dpm/100 cm^2}} may be opened if other criteria are satisfied.
fields.Extra: Q17. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. According to the ABR RISC study guide, an external wipe test of a transported package of Tl-201 should not exceed 20,000 dpm/100cm^2.

fields.Text: A Tl-201 package wipe test of 875 dpm/100 cm^2 is below the {{c1::20,000 dpm/100 cm^2}} action threshold, so the package can be opened.
fields.Extra: Q17. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. According to the ABR RISC study guide, an external wipe test of a transported package of Tl-201 should not exceed 20,000 dpm/100cm^2.

fields.Text:
Older patient with dysphagia and regurgitation of undigested food. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Zenker diverticulum}}
fields.Extra: Q18. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: The most common complication of a Zenker's diverticulum is aspiration, which can cause pneumonia.

fields.Text: The most common complication of Zenker diverticulum is {{c1::aspiration}}.
fields.Extra: Q18. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: The most common complication of a Zenker's diverticulum is aspiration, which can cause pneumonia.

fields.Text: On ACR Appropriateness Criteria, an examination rated `usually appropriate` receives a score of {{c1::7 to 9}}.
fields.Extra: Q19. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: The ACR appropriateness criteria are based on clinical conditions and associated modifiers. Each clinical condition includes three variants based on patient factors and symptoms. For each variant, each possible imaging modality is rated on a 1 (low) to 9 (high) scale based on the appropriateness of the modality for the variant under discussion.

fields.Text:
Patient with DISH and progressive spastic paresis. What additional abnormality is shown?

{{c1::Ossification of the posterior longitudinal ligament}}
fields.Extra: Q20. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Ossification of the posterior longitudinal ligament (OPLL) is often associated with several other entities, including diffuse idiopathic skeletal hyperostosis (DISH), ossification of the ligamentum flavum, and ankylosing spondylitis.

fields.Text: DISH is commonly associated with ossification of the {{c1::posterior longitudinal ligament (OPLL)}}.
fields.Extra: Q20. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Ossification of the posterior longitudinal ligament (OPLL) is often associated with several other entities, including diffuse idiopathic skeletal hyperostosis (DISH), ossification of the ligamentum flavum, and ankylosing spondylitis.

fields.Text: After I-131 administration, breastfeeding should be discontinued for {{c1::the current child}}.
fields.Extra: Q21. Correct; difficulty: easy. For 131I, recommendations are that the patient should not resume breastfeeding for the current child but may breastfeed babies from subsequent pregnancies.

fields.Text:
Acute severe headache with the imaging findings shown. What vascular lesion caused the hemorrhage?

{{c1::Intracranial aneurysm}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q22. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Intracranial aneurysm with bilateral renal cysts is highly suspicious for autosomal dominant polycystic kidney disease.

fields.Text: An intracranial aneurysm with numerous bilateral renal cysts suggests {{c1::autosomal dominant polycystic kidney disease}}.
fields.Extra: Q22. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Intracranial aneurysm with bilateral renal cysts is highly suspicious for autosomal dominant polycystic kidney disease.

fields.Text:
History of miscarriage with uterine MRI shown. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Bicornuate uterus}}
fields.Extra: Q23. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The axial T2 MRI shows a cleft greater than 1 cm in depth (green arrow) in the external contour of the uterine fundus.

fields.Text: A uterine anomaly with an external fundal cleft greater than 1 cm is characteristic of a {{c1::bicornuate uterus}}.
fields.Extra: Q23. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The axial T2 MRI shows a cleft greater than 1 cm in depth (green arrow) in the external contour of the uterine fundus.

fields.Text: Patients are typically screened for MR safety issues in {{c1::Zone II}}.
fields.Extra: Q24. Correct; difficulty: easy. There are several safety measures involved in the use of magnetic resonance (MR) scanners, primarily due to the scanners’ strong magnetic fields.

fields.Text: The ACR adult abdominal CTDIvol reference value is {{c1::25 mGy}} using a 32 cm phantom.
fields.Extra: Q25. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: CTDIvol for adult abdominal CT has a reference value of 25 mGy and pass/fail threshold of 30 mGy measured using a 32 cm phantom, balancing image quality with radiation safety.

fields.Text:
What named radiographic finding indicates a remote MCL injury?

{{c1::Pellegrini-Stieda lesion}}
fields.Extra: Q26. Correct; difficulty: easy. The finding in this case is a Pellegrini-Stieda lesion, which is a vertically-oriented calcification at the proximal aspect of the MCL at its femoral attachment (yellow arrow).

fields.Text: A Pellegrini-Stieda lesion represents chronic injury of the {{c1::medial collateral ligament (MCL)}} at its femoral attachment.
fields.Extra: Q26. Correct; difficulty: easy. The finding in this case is a Pellegrini-Stieda lesion, which is a vertically-oriented calcification at the proximal aspect of the MCL at its femoral attachment (yellow arrow).

fields.Text: The maximum annual total effective dose equivalent for a radiation worker under age 18 is {{c1::5 mSv}}.
fields.Extra: Q27. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: The NRC annual occupational dose limit for minors is 10% of adult dose limits. The adult limit is 50 mSv (0.05 Sv/5 rem), so the limit for minors is 5 mSv (0.005 Sv/0.5 rem).

fields.Text:
Is the tunneled hemodialysis catheter position shown acceptable?

{{c1::Yes; the functional tip is appropriately positioned in the right atrium}}
fields.Extra: Q28. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Hemodialysis dual-lumen catheters have staggered tips to prevent recirculation, with the venous return port positioned more inferior than the arterial port. Tips should be placed in the caudal superior vena cava or right atrium via right internal jugular approach for optimal flow rates and reduced complications.

fields.Text: A tunneled hemodialysis catheter is appropriately positioned when its staggered functional tip lies in the {{c1::right atrium without contacting the atrial floor}}.
fields.Extra: Q28. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Hemodialysis dual-lumen catheters have staggered tips to prevent recirculation, with the venous return port positioned more inferior than the arterial port. Tips should be placed in the caudal superior vena cava or right atrium via right internal jugular approach for optimal flow rates and reduced complications.

fields.Text: A tunneled hemodialysis catheter with staggered tips in the right atrium is {{c1::appropriately positioned}}, not too deep solely because it extends below the cavoatrial junction.
fields.Extra: Q28. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Hemodialysis dual-lumen catheters have staggered tips to prevent recirculation, with the venous return port positioned more inferior than the arterial port. Tips should be placed in the caudal superior vena cava or right atrium via right internal jugular approach for optimal flow rates and reduced complications.

fields.Text:
RUQ pain with this gallbladder ultrasound finding. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Adenomyomatosis}}
fields.Extra: Q29. Correct; difficulty: easy. The image presented shows a focal mass in the gallbladder wall (red arrow) with echogenic intramural foci with associated comet tail artifacts (yellow arrows).

fields.Text: Gallbladder wall echogenic foci with comet-tail artifact are highly specific for {{c1::adenomyomatosis}}.
fields.Extra: Q29. Correct; difficulty: easy. The image presented shows a focal mass in the gallbladder wall (red arrow) with echogenic intramural foci with associated comet tail artifacts (yellow arrows).

fields.Text: In ACR TI-RADS, punctate echogenic foci receive {{c1::3 points}} and are the most suspicious individual thyroid nodule feature.
fields.Extra: Q30. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Punctate echogenic foci carry the highest point value (3 points) in ACR TI-RADS, making them the most suspicious individual ultrasound finding for thyroid malignancy, capable of elevating otherwise low-suspicion nodules to TR4 category.

fields.Text:
Large adrenal mass with central necrosis and venous tumor thrombus. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Adrenal cortical carcinoma}}

1/5
fields.Extra: Q31. Correct; difficulty: easy. The images show a very large right adrenal mass (blue arrow) with central necrosis and tumor thrombus (purple arrow) invading the IVC.

fields.Text: A large adrenal mass with central necrosis and IVC tumor thrombus strongly suggests {{c1::adrenal cortical carcinoma}}.
fields.Extra: Q31. Correct; difficulty: easy. The images show a very large right adrenal mass (blue arrow) with central necrosis and tumor thrombus (purple arrow) invading the IVC.

fields.Text: Prospective ECG-gated cardiac MRI acquisition is triggered by the {{c1::R wave}}.
fields.Extra: Q32. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: In prospective ECG-Gated cardiac MRI, data acquisition is triggered by each R-wave and is stopped after the data of the estimated number of cardiac phases has been collected. This acquisition scheme results in a small-time interval of no data collection.

fields.Text: Prospective ECG-gated cardiac MRI begins acquisition at each {{c1::R wave}}, not the S wave.
fields.Extra: Q32. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: In prospective ECG-Gated cardiac MRI, data acquisition is triggered by each R-wave and is stopped after the data of the estimated number of cardiac phases has been collected. This acquisition scheme results in a small-time interval of no data collection.

fields.Text:
Which cardiac valve has been replaced by the marked prosthesis?

{{c1::Tricuspid valve}}
fields.Extra: Q33. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Cardiac valves have distinct radiographic positions: pulmonic valve is most superior in both frontal and lateral views, aortic valve is located superiorly appearing in profile frontally and anteriorly laterally, mitral valve is positioned inferiorly appearing en face frontally and in profile laterally, and tricuspid valve is most inferior appearing anteriorly on lateral view.

fields.Text: A prosthetic valve in the most inferior anterior valve position on the lateral radiograph represents the {{c1::tricuspid valve}}.
fields.Extra: Q33. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Cardiac valves have distinct radiographic positions: pulmonic valve is most superior in both frontal and lateral views, aortic valve is located superiorly appearing in profile frontally and anteriorly laterally, mitral valve is positioned inferiorly appearing en face frontally and in profile laterally, and tricuspid valve is most inferior appearing anteriorly on lateral view.

fields.Text: A valve prosthesis in the low anterior right-heart position represents the {{c1::tricuspid valve}}, rather than the mitral valve.
fields.Extra: Q33. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Cardiac valves have distinct radiographic positions: pulmonic valve is most superior in both frontal and lateral views, aortic valve is located superiorly appearing in profile frontally and anteriorly laterally, mitral valve is positioned inferiorly appearing en face frontally and in profile laterally, and tricuspid valve is most inferior appearing anteriorly on lateral view.

fields.Text:
During uterine artery embolization, what collateral vessel supplying fibroids is shown?

{{c1::Ovarian artery}}
fields.Extra: Q34. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The diagnostic angiogram shows bilateral ovarian artery enlargement and tortuosity (red arrows).

fields.Text: Embolization of an enlarged ovarian artery that supplies uterine fibroids risks {{c1::ovarian failure}}.
fields.Extra: Q34. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The diagnostic angiogram shows bilateral ovarian artery enlargement and tortuosity (red arrows).

fields.Text:
What skull-base foramen is indicated?

{{c1::Foramen rotundum}}
fields.Extra: Q35. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Foramen rotundum is the most medial foramen in the greater sphenoid wing, closest to the sphenoid body.

fields.Text: The most medial foramen of the greater sphenoid wing, adjacent to the sphenoid body, is the {{c1::foramen rotundum}}.
fields.Extra: Q35. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Foramen rotundum is the most medial foramen in the greater sphenoid wing, closest to the sphenoid body.

fields.Text:
Coagulopathic bone marrow transplant patient undergoing hepatic evaluation. What procedure is shown?

{{c1::Transjugular hepatic biopsy}}
fields.Extra: Q36. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The catheter is positioned in the right hepatic vein (red arrow).

fields.Text: When hepatic biopsy is required in a patient with thrombocytopenia or elevated INR, the preferred approach is {{c1::transjugular hepatic biopsy}}.
fields.Extra: Q36. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The catheter is positioned in the right hepatic vein (red arrow).

fields.Text: The periprocedural practice that most reduces iatrogenic infection risk is {{c1::hand washing}}.
fields.Extra: Q37. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Hand washing is a simple yet very effective way to reduce risk of iatrogenic infection.

fields.Text:
Bodybuilder with multiple hyperenhancing hepatic masses containing fat. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Hepatocellular adenomas}}

1/2
fields.Extra: Q38. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Hepatocellular adenomas appearing heterogeneous on CT in patients with anabolic steroid use should raise suspicion for beta-catenin mutated subtype, which carries the highest malignant transformation risk requiring surgical resection regardless of size.

fields.Text: Hepatocellular adenomas in patients using anabolic steroids are {{c1::hormonally responsive}} and may carry malignant transformation risk.
fields.Extra: Q38. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Hepatocellular adenomas appearing heterogeneous on CT in patients with anabolic steroid use should raise suspicion for beta-catenin mutated subtype, which carries the highest malignant transformation risk requiring surgical resection regardless of size.

fields.Text:
Sudden severe headache with this noncontrast head CT. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Subarachnoid hemorrhage}}
fields.Extra: Q39. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The non-contrast enhanced CT image shows a large volume of subarachnoid hemorrhage (SAH) within the basal cisterns (red arrow), as well as a large intraventricular component (orange arrow).

fields.Text: Approximately 85% of spontaneous subarachnoid hemorrhages are due to a {{c1::ruptured aneurysm}}.
fields.Extra: Q39. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The non-contrast enhanced CT image shows a large volume of subarachnoid hemorrhage (SAH) within the basal cisterns (red arrow), as well as a large intraventricular component (orange arrow).

fields.Text:
What disease produces the femoral-head abnormality shown?

{{c1::Legg-Calve-Perthes disease}}
fields.Extra: Q40. Correct; difficulty: easy. This is a classic finding of Legg-Calve-Perthes disease (avascular necrosis of the proximal femoral head).

fields.Text: Avascular necrosis of the proximal femoral head in a child is characteristic of {{c1::Legg-Calve-Perthes disease}}.
fields.Extra: Q40. Correct; difficulty: easy. This is a classic finding of Legg-Calve-Perthes disease (avascular necrosis of the proximal femoral head).

fields.Text: Coordinating care across multiple settings and sites is an example of {{c1::systems-based practice}}.
fields.Extra: Q41. Correct; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Six core competencies of maintenance of certification (MOC) are patient care, medical knowledge, interpersonal and communication skills, professionalism, systems-based practice, practice-based learning and improvement.

fields.Text: Among gadolinium-based contrast agents, the macrocyclic agent {{c1::gadobutrol (Gadavist)}} has a very low NSF risk.
fields.Extra: Q42. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Nephrogenic systemic fibrosis occurs almost exclusively in patients with renal impairment and is associated with administration of gadolinium-based contrast agents (GBCAs) used in MRI. The stability of the gadolinium chelate is the major determinant of the risk of NSF. The most important feature of a stable chelate is a macrocyclic structure, found in gadobutrol (Gadavist).

fields.Text: NSF risk is lowest with stable macrocyclic gadolinium agents such as {{c1::gadobutrol (Gadavist)}}, not linear agents such as gadodiamide.
fields.Extra: Q42. Incorrect; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Nephrogenic systemic fibrosis occurs almost exclusively in patients with renal impairment and is associated with administration of gadolinium-based contrast agents (GBCAs) used in MRI. The stability of the gadolinium chelate is the major determinant of the risk of NSF. The most important feature of a stable chelate is a macrocyclic structure, found in gadobutrol (Gadavist).

fields.Text: Failing to stay current with advances in imaging technology violates the responsibility of {{c1::professional competence}}.
fields.Extra: Q43. Correct; difficulty: easy. The radiologist’s failure to stay abreast of the most recent innovations in medical imaging signifies a violation of professional competence.

fields.Text:
36-year-old man with back pain and an enhancing conus-region mass. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Myxopapillary ependymoma}}

1/4
fields.Extra: Q44. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The images show a heterogeneously enhancing mass arising from the region of the conus, which fills the canal.

fields.Text: An enhancing mass arising in the conus/filum terminale region is most commonly a {{c1::myxopapillary ependymoma}}.
fields.Extra: Q44. Correct; difficulty: moderate. The images show a heterogeneously enhancing mass arising from the region of the conus, which fills the canal.

fields.Text:
What benign breast process causes the rim/eggshell calcifications shown?

{{c1::Fat necrosis with oil cysts}}
fields.Extra: Q45. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Oil cysts are round radiolucent masses on mammography indicating benign fat necrosis, but irregular rim calcification or collapse can contribute to suspicious appearances requiring biopsy.

fields.Text: Benign rim or eggshell breast calcifications in oil cysts/fat necrosis are commonly sequelae of {{c1::trauma}}.
fields.Extra: Q45. Correct; difficulty: easy. Vital Concept: Oil cysts are round radiolucent masses on mammography indicating benign fat necrosis, but irregular rim calcification or collapse can contribute to suspicious appearances requiring biopsy.

fields.Text:
Sellar mass with hypercalcemia and elevated PTH. What syndrome is suggested?

{{c1::MEN1}}
fields.Extra: Q46. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Multiple endocrine neoplasia type 1 (MEN1), also known as Wermer syndrome, is an autosomal dominant genetic disease that results in proliferative lesions in multiple endocrine organs, particularly the pituitary gland, islet cells of the pancreas and parathyroid glands.

fields.Text: MEN1, suggested by a pituitary mass with primary hyperparathyroidism, is inherited in an {{c1::autosomal dominant}} pattern.
fields.Extra: Q46. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Multiple endocrine neoplasia type 1 (MEN1), also known as Wermer syndrome, is an autosomal dominant genetic disease that results in proliferative lesions in multiple endocrine organs, particularly the pituitary gland, islet cells of the pancreas and parathyroid glands.

fields.Text:
Incidental apparent narrowing of the distal aortic arch without symptoms. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Aortic pseudocoarctation}}
fields.Extra: Q47. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: With phase contrast imaging, MRI is a viable modality to assess hemodynamic significance of aortic pseudocoarctation.

fields.Text: Phase-contrast MRI without contrast can determine the hemodynamic significance of {{c1::aortic pseudocoarctation}}.
fields.Extra: Q47. Correct; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: With phase contrast imaging, MRI is a viable modality to assess hemodynamic significance of aortic pseudocoarctation.

fields.Text:
Palpable superficial breast mass with the ultrasound appearance shown. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::Epidermal inclusion cyst}}
fields.Extra: Q48. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Epidermal inclusion cysts appear as well-circumscribed hypoechoic masses in subcutaneous tissue with posterior acoustic enhancement, internal echogenic debris from keratin contents, and characteristic connection to skin surface (punctum), remaining avascular on Doppler imaging.

fields.Text: A superficial epidermal inclusion cyst of the breast is a benign finding reported as {{c1::BI-RADS 2}}.
fields.Extra: Q48. Correct; difficulty: moderate. Vital Concept: Epidermal inclusion cysts appear as well-circumscribed hypoechoic masses in subcutaneous tissue with posterior acoustic enhancement, internal echogenic debris from keratin contents, and characteristic connection to skin surface (punctum), remaining avascular on Doppler imaging.

fields.Text: In a patient with LBBB undergoing myocardial perfusion imaging, use {{c1::vasodilator stress such as regadenoson}} rather than exercise or dobutamine.
fields.Extra: Q49. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Vasodilator stress testing (regadenoson, adenosine, or dipyridamole) with nuclear perfusion imaging is optimal for left bundle branch block patients because exercise worsens abnormal septal wall motion and creates false-positive septal perfusion defects due to abnormal electrical activation.

fields.Text: For myocardial perfusion imaging in LBBB, choose a {{c1::vasodilator stress agent such as regadenoson}} rather than dobutamine, which can aggravate false-positive septal defects.
fields.Extra: Q49. Incorrect; difficulty: hard. Vital Concept: Vasodilator stress testing (regadenoson, adenosine, or dipyridamole) with nuclear perfusion imaging is optimal for left bundle branch block patients because exercise worsens abnormal septal wall motion and creates false-positive septal perfusion defects due to abnormal electrical activation.

fields.Text:
Recurrent left lower extremity DVT with this venographic appearance. Most likely diagnosis?

{{c1::May-Thurner syndrome}}
fields.Extra: Q50. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Treatment requires catheter-directed thrombolysis to dissolve acute clot and stenting to address underlying mechanical compression. IVC filter is commonly placed for embolic protection, followed by its retrieval after successful treatment. Stenting treats the anatomic problem while thrombolysis addresses the thrombotic component.

fields.Text: Symptomatic May-Thurner syndrome with acute thrombus is treated with {{c1::catheter-directed thrombolysis followed by self-expandable venous stenting}}.
fields.Extra: Q50. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Treatment requires catheter-directed thrombolysis to dissolve acute clot and stenting to address underlying mechanical compression. IVC filter is commonly placed for embolic protection, followed by its retrieval after successful treatment. Stenting treats the anatomic problem while thrombolysis addresses the thrombotic component.

fields.Text: May-Thurner syndrome with recurrent DVT requires thrombus clearance followed by {{c1::self-expandable venous stenting}}, rather than primary balloon-expandable stenting.
fields.Extra: Q50. Incorrect; difficulty: not listed. Vital Concept: Treatment requires catheter-directed thrombolysis to dissolve acute clot and stenting to address underlying mechanical compression. IVC filter is commonly placed for embolic protection, followed by its retrieval after successful treatment. Stenting treats the anatomic problem while thrombolysis addresses the thrombotic component.
